compounds.name: BPC-157

administration.frequency: 1-2x daily

compounds.mechanism_summary: Upregulates VEGFR2 (angiogenesis), modulates nitric oxide, interacts with dopamine system. Promotes tendon/ligament fibroblast migration and collagen synthesis.

evidence.detail: 50+ rat/mouse studies showing tendon, ligament, muscle, and GI healing. Zero human RCTs. All human evidence is anecdotal/community-reported.

regulatory.note: Included in 14 peptides expected to return to Category 1 per RFK Feb 2026 announcement — status pending

community_insights.top_positive_use: Tendon/ligament healing — highest volume of positive reports
community_insights.top_negative_report: Anxiety/mood disruption — frequently reported, often unexpected
community_insights.common_mistake: Using for gut healing but not monitoring mood/sleep changes
community_insights.pro_tip: Start low (150mcg), monitor mood daily for first 2 weeks before increasing

compounds.name: Semaglutide

administration.frequency: 1x weekly (SubQ), daily (oral)

compounds.mechanism_summary: GLP-1 receptor agonist. Mimics incretin hormone to reduce appetite, slow gastric emptying, enhance insulin secretion, and reduce glucagon. Acts on hypothalamic appetite centers.

evidence.detail: Multiple Phase 3 RCTs (STEP, SUSTAIN trials). FDA-approved for T2 diabetes and obesity. Strong efficacy data: 15-17% weight loss in trials.

regulatory.note: FDA-approved as Ozempic (diabetes) and Wegovy (obesity). Compounded versions available but regulatory status contested.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Sustained weight loss with appetite suppression
community_insights.top_negative_report: Hair loss (FAERS signal ROR 2.46), gastroparesis, muscle loss (30% of weight lost is lean mass)
community_insights.common_mistake: Not doing resistance training while on GLP-1 — accelerates muscle loss
community_insights.pro_tip: Titrate slowly (start 0.25mg, increase every 4 weeks). Prioritize protein (1g/lb) and lift weights.

compounds.name: Tirzepatide

administration.frequency: 1x weekly

compounds.mechanism_summary: Dual GLP-1 and GIP receptor agonist. Combines incretin effects of both pathways for enhanced appetite suppression, improved insulin sensitivity, and glucose-dependent insulin secretion.

evidence.detail: Multiple Phase 3 RCTs (SURMOUNT, SURPASS trials). FDA-approved for T2 diabetes and obesity. 20-26% weight loss in trials — superior to semaglutide.

regulatory.note: FDA-approved as Mounjaro (diabetes) and Zepbound (obesity). Compounded versions widely used for cost savings.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Weight loss superior to semaglutide with reportedly fewer GI side effects
community_insights.top_negative_report: Sulfur burps, severe GI distress when switching from branded to compounded, injection site reactions
community_insights.common_mistake: Switching from branded (Zepbound) to compounded without expecting different side effect profiles
community_insights.pro_tip: If switching sources, treat it like starting a new compound — monitor closely for 7 days.

compounds.name: Retatrutide

administration.frequency: 1x weekly

compounds.mechanism_summary: Triple agonist: GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors. The glucagon component adds thermogenic fat burning on top of appetite suppression. Most potent weight loss peptide in trials.

evidence.detail: Phase 2 data: 28.7% weight loss at 48 weeks (highest of any obesity drug). Phase 3 trials ongoing. Not yet FDA-approved.

regulatory.note: Phase 3 trials by Eli Lilly. Gray market availability ahead of approval. Expected to return to Category 1 per RFK announcement.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Dramatic weight loss exceeding semaglutide/tirzepatide results
community_insights.top_negative_report: Sleep disruption (~8% of users), emotional blunting, suspected fake/underdosed products
community_insights.common_mistake: Starting at high dose — titrate from 1mg. Product authenticity is a major concern with gray market sources
community_insights.pro_tip: If sleep disruption occurs, it's likely dose-dependent and may resolve at lower doses. Overnight glucose dips triggering cortisol is the suspected mechanism.

compounds.name: TB-500

administration.frequency: 2x weekly (loading), 1x weekly (maintenance)

compounds.mechanism_summary: Promotes cellular migration and angiogenesis. Upregulates actin for cell motility, enabling tissue repair. Systemic distribution — works throughout the body rather than locally.

evidence.detail: Animal studies showing wound healing, cardiac repair, and neurological protection. No human RCTs. Fragile peptide bond — degradation is a significant real-world issue.

regulatory.note: Included in 14 peptides expected to return to Category 1 per RFK Feb 2026 announcement — status pending

community_insights.top_positive_use: Systemic healing when stacked with BPC-157 — the 'Wolverine Protocol'
community_insights.top_negative_report: Tumor growth concern (promotes angiogenesis which could feed dormant tumors), paradoxical joint pain
community_insights.common_mistake: Storing at room temperature — TB-500 has a fragile peptide bond and degrades faster than most peptides. Keep cold.
community_insights.pro_tip: Split weekly dose into 2 injections for more stable blood levels instead of one large loading dose.

compounds.name: CJC-1295

administration.frequency: 1x daily (before bed)

compounds.mechanism_summary: Growth hormone releasing hormone (GHRH) analog. Stimulates pituitary to release GH in pulsatile pattern mimicking natural secretion. DAC version has extended half-life.

evidence.detail: Human pharmacokinetic data exists for the GHRH analog. Limited clinical efficacy data for bodybuilding/anti-aging uses. Well-characterized mechanism.

regulatory.note: Research chemical status. Often sold as CJC-1295 with or without DAC — different pharmacokinetics.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Sleep quality improvement and body recomposition when stacked with Ipamorelin
community_insights.top_negative_report: Water retention/edema, lower back pain (GH-mediated), elevated GH effects worse in older users
community_insights.common_mistake: Taking with food — GH release is blunted by insulin. Take fasted, ideally before bed.
community_insights.pro_tip: The CJC+IPA stack is the most well-characterized GH protocol. Start at 100mcg each before bed.

compounds.name: Ipamorelin

administration.frequency: 1x daily (before bed)

compounds.mechanism_summary: Growth hormone secretagogue — mimics ghrelin to trigger GH release from the pituitary. Selective for GH (doesn't significantly raise cortisol or prolactin like other GHRPs).

evidence.detail: Human PK data and some clinical studies. Cleanest side effect profile of the GHRP family. Well-understood mechanism but limited efficacy data for anti-aging uses.

regulatory.note: Research chemical. Often blended with CJC-1295 in same vial by compounding sources.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Sleep quality — most consistent positive report. Clean GH release without hunger spikes (unlike GHRP-2/6)
community_insights.top_negative_report: Mild — headache, dizziness, transient flushing. Considered the safest GH secretagogue.
community_insights.common_mistake: Expecting dramatic body composition changes from IPA alone — it's subtle and best as part of a stack
community_insights.pro_tip: Commonly blended with CJC-1295 in same vial. If blended, reconstitute as one — don't try to separate.

compounds.name: GHK-Cu

administration.frequency: 1x daily

compounds.mechanism_summary: Naturally occurring copper-binding peptide. Stimulates collagen, elastin, and glycosaminoglycan synthesis. Promotes wound healing, skin remodeling, and has anti-inflammatory properties.

evidence.detail: Solid in-vitro and animal data for wound healing and collagen synthesis. Topical use has more evidence than systemic SubQ. Anti-aging claims largely unvalidated beyond skin.

regulatory.note: Available as research chemical (SubQ) and in cosmetic products (topical). No FDA approval for any indication.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Skin quality improvement (topical) and joint pain relief when stacked with BPC-157/TB-500
community_insights.top_negative_report: Skin irritation when combined with retinol/retinoids, overhyped for systemic anti-aging
community_insights.common_mistake: Expecting systemic anti-aging results from topical application — SubQ and topical have very different bioavailability
community_insights.pro_tip: If using topically alongside retinol, alternate days — don't apply both at the same time.

compounds.name: Tesamorelin

administration.frequency: 1x daily

compounds.mechanism_summary: GHRH analog — stimulates pulsatile GH and IGF-1 release through the natural GHRH pathway. Specifically targets visceral adipose tissue reduction.

evidence.detail: FDA-approved for HIV-associated lipodystrophy (Egrifta). RCTs demonstrate significant visceral fat reduction. Off-label use for body composition is common but less studied.

regulatory.note: FDA-approved as Egrifta for HIV lipodystrophy. Off-label and compounded versions used for body composition.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Visceral fat reduction — the most targeted fat-loss peptide available
community_insights.top_negative_report: Joint pain (1-10% incidence, dose-dependent, GH-mediated), carpal tunnel syndrome, fluid retention
community_insights.common_mistake: Stacking tesamorelin with CJC-1295 — redundant GHRH pathway stimulation, no added benefit, more side effects
community_insights.pro_tip: Joint pain is dose-dependent. If it appears, reducing dose often resolves it. Don't stack with other GHRH analogs.

compounds.name: Thymosin Alpha-1

administration.frequency: 2x weekly

compounds.mechanism_summary: Immune modulator derived from thymic protein. Enhances T-cell maturation and function, promotes dendritic cell activation, and modulates cytokine production. Balances immune response rather than simply boosting it.

evidence.detail: Approved in 35+ countries (as Zadaxin) for hepatitis B/C. Clinical trial data exists for immune modulation. Not FDA-approved in the US but has more clinical evidence than most peptides in this list.

regulatory.note: Approved internationally (Zadaxin) but not by FDA. Orphan drug status was explored. Available through compounding pharmacies.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Immune modulation for autoimmune conditions (Hashimoto's thyroiditis, chronic infections)
community_insights.top_negative_report: Generally well-tolerated. Occasional flu-like symptoms in first few doses as immune system activates.
community_insights.common_mistake: Expecting immediate results — immune modulation takes weeks to months to show effects
community_insights.pro_tip: One of the few peptides with actual clinical data across multiple countries. Often pre-dosed at 1.6mg per vial — one vial = one injection.

compounds.name: Semax

administration.frequency: 1-2x daily

compounds.mechanism_summary: Synthetic analog of ACTH(4-10). Modulates BDNF expression, enhances serotonergic and dopaminergic neurotransmission. Neuroprotective and nootropic effects via melanocortin pathway.

evidence.detail: Approved in Russia and Ukraine for stroke recovery and cognitive enhancement. Russian clinical data exists but limited Western peer review. Mechanism is well-characterized.

regulatory.note: Approved in Russia/Ukraine. No FDA approval or trials. Available as research chemical in the West.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Cognitive clarity and focus — often described as 'cleaner' than stimulants
community_insights.top_negative_report: Tolerance development with daily use, nasal irritation, limited Western evidence base
community_insights.common_mistake: Using continuously without breaks — tolerance builds. Cycle 5 days on, 2 days off.
community_insights.pro_tip: Intranasal is the standard route. If reconstituting from powder, use a nasal atomizer. Most people buy pre-mixed sprays.

compounds.name: Selank

administration.frequency: 1-2x daily

compounds.mechanism_summary: Synthetic analog of tuftsin (immune peptide). Anxiolytic effects via GABA modulation without sedation or dependence. Also modulates BDNF and serotonin. Dual immune-modulating and anxiolytic properties.

evidence.detail: Approved in Russia for anxiety and neurasthenia. Clinical data shows anxiolytic effects comparable to benzodiazepines without sedation or dependence. Limited Western validation.

regulatory.note: Approved in Russia for generalized anxiety. No FDA approval or trials. Available as research chemical.

community_insights.top_positive_use: Anxiety reduction without sedation — popular among those avoiding benzodiazepines or SSRIs
community_insights.top_negative_report: Tolerance questions with prolonged use, sedation at higher doses, limited evidence outside Russian studies
community_insights.common_mistake: Treating it like a benzo replacement without understanding the evidence gap between Russian approval standards and FDA standards
community_insights.pro_tip: Often stacked with Semax (Selank for calm, Semax for focus). Same intranasal delivery. Cycle with breaks.

evidence.detail: FDA-approved. 8 PubMed results found, 1 clinical studies.

community_insights.pro_tip: Daily injection vs semaglutide's weekly. Consider switching to semaglutide for convenience unless liraglutide is specifically indicated.
